questions.text: The size of a file can be measured in different units. State how many bits there are in a byte.
The size of a file can be measured in different units. State how many kibibytes (KiB) there are in 2 mebibytes (MiB).
The size of a file can be measured in different units. Identify the unit of data that is equal to 4 bits.
An image has a 16-bit colour depth and a resolution of 1000 × 1750 pixels. State what is meant by a 16-bit colour depth.

marking_points.text: 16 bits used to represent each colour in the image

questions.text: An image has a 16-bit colour depth and a resolution of 1000 × 1750 pixels. The resolution of the image is changed to 750 × 1500 pixels. Give the effect this will have on the file size of the image.

A programmer is creating a computer game. One character is not moving correctly. The programmer needs to debug the program. The addresses are displayed as hexadecimal numbers. One address is A2F. Convert the address to binary.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct nibble, in the correct order.

questions.text: A programmer is creating a computer game. One character is not moving correctly. The programmer needs to debug the program. The addresses are displayed as hexadecimal numbers. One address is A2F. Convert the address to denary.

A programmer is creating a computer game. One character is not moving correctly. The programmer needs to debug the program. The binary number stored for another address is 000110011011. Convert the binary number to hexadecimal.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct character, in the correct order.

questions.text: A programmer is creating a computer game. One character is not moving correctly. The programmer needs to debug the program. The binary number stored for another address is 000110011011. Convert the binary number to denary.

A programmer is creating a computer game. One character is not moving correctly. The programmer needs to debug the program. The addresses are displayed as hexadecimal numbers. Give one reason why the addresses are displayed in hexadecimal instead of binary.

marking_points.text: It is a shorter representation of binary // It takes up less screen space

questions.text:
A programmer is creating a computer game. Identify two other ways that hexadecimal is used in computer science.
1
2

A programmer is creating a computer game. The health value for a character in the computer game can sometimes be a negative value. The negative values are stored as two's complement 8-bit integers. A character has a health value of –25. Calculate the two's complement 8-bit integer for –25. Show all your working.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for correct working, one mark for correct answer.

marking_points.text: One mark for correct working: Example: flip and add

questions.text:
The table contains statements about types of secondary storage. Complete the table by writing the correct type of secondary storage for each statement.

Statements:
- Pits and lands are created on a reflective surface.
- NAND or NOR technology is used.
- Platters are spun that are divided into tracks and sectors.
- Electromagnets are used to read and write data.
- Transistors are used as control gates and floating gates.
- A red or blue laser is used to read and write data.

visuals.description: Two-column table with 'type of secondary storage' and 'statement' headers; 6 rows with statements pre-filled and type column blank

rows.definition: Pits and lands are created on a reflective surface.
NAND or NOR technology is used.
Platters are spun that are divided into tracks and sectors.
Electromagnets are used to read and write data.
Transistors are used as control gates and floating gates.
A red or blue laser is used to read and write data.

visuals.description: Two-column table with 'type of secondary storage' and 'statement' headers; 6 rows with statements pre-filled and type column blank

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct type of storage.

marking_points.text: optical — Pits and lands are created on a reflective surface.
solid-state — NAND or NOR technology is used.
magnetic — Platters are spun that are divided into tracks and sectors.
magnetic — Electromagnets are used to read and write data.
solid-state — Transistors are used as control gates and floating gates.
optical — A red or blue laser is used to read and write data.

questions.text:
A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and read only memory (ROM). Tick (✓) one box to show which statement is correct about RAM.
A It is non-volatile storage.
B It stores the bootloader.
C It stores data that is currently in use.
D It is not directly accessible by the central processing unit (CPU).

visuals.description: Four options A-D with tick boxes

rows: C It stores data that is currently in use.
D It is not directly accessible by the central processing unit (CPU).

questions.text: A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and read only memory (ROM). ROM is a type of primary storage. State the characteristic of ROM that makes it a type of primary storage.

marking_points.text: It is directly accessible by the CPU

questions.text:
A sensor is an input device that is used to capture data from its surrounding environment. Circle three other input devices.
touch screen    hard disk drive (HDD)    headphones
speaker    microphone    actuator
universal serial bus (USB) drive    printer    keyboard

A sensor is an input device that is used to capture data from its surrounding environment. Sensors can be used in an automated system. Give two benefits of using sensors in this type of system.
1
2

marking_points.text: Data can be collected without human intervention
Gathers data faster than a human
It will be accurate at taking readings
It can take readings in dangerous environments

questions.text:
A sensor is an input device that is used to capture data from its surrounding environment. The table contains different types of sensors and a use for each. Complete the table by giving a suitable use for each type of sensor. The first one has been done for you.

Types of sensor: acoustic (example given: to monitor whether a water pipe has cracked and is leaking and dripping onto the floor), temperature, humidity, infra-red, magnetic field.

visuals.description: Two-column table with 'type of sensor' and 'use' headers; acoustic row pre-filled as example; temperature, humidity, infra-red and magnetic field rows blank

rows.definition: to monitor whether a water pipe has cracked and is leaking and dripping onto the floor

visuals.description: Two-column table with 'type of sensor' and 'use' headers; acoustic row pre-filled as example; temperature, humidity, infra-red and magnetic field rows blank

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each use (Max 1) per sensor:

marking_points.text: Temperature: Checking whether the water in a kettle is boiling // Monitoring the temperature in a room that is climate controlled
Humidity: Checking whether the air is dry enough in a spray-painting garage // Checking whether the air is moist enough in a greenhouse
Infra-red: Detecting motion in a room for a security system // Detecting whether a person is approaching automatic doors
Magnetic field: Counting vehicles that cross a bridge // Monitoring vehicles that enter a car park

questions.text: A company has a network that includes a web server. Data is transmitted across the network using serial half-duplex data transmission. Draw and annotate a diagram to show how the data is transmitted using serial half-duplex data transmission.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct part of the diagram.

marking_points.text: Bits beings sent one at a time
Bits being sent over a single wire
Data can be sent to and from the web server/network component/computers …

questions.text:
A company has a network that includes a web server. Data is transmitted across the network using serial half-duplex data transmission. Give two benefits of the company using serial half-duplex data transmission for this purpose.
1
2
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Bits will not be skewed // Bits are sent in order
Data can be sent over a long distance (if needed)
It is possible to download and upload data to the web server

questions.text: A company has a network that includes a web server. Data is transmitted across the network using serial half-duplex data transmission. Give one drawback of the company using serial half-duplex data transmission for this purpose.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: The transmission of data may be relatively slow
Data cannot be sent and received at the same time

questions.text:
A company has a network that includes a web server. The company uses a proxy server to help protect the web server and the network from cyber security threats. Give three cyber security threats that the proxy server can help protect against.
1
2
3

A company has a network that includes a web server. The company uses a proxy server to help protect the web server and the network from cyber security threats. Identify two functions of the proxy server that can be used to help protect the web server and the network.
1
2

marking_points.text: Can limit the number of requests sent to the web server at a time
Can process common requests that will not need to enter the network
Examine incoming data to the webserver/network
Can have set rules/criteria for data to meet
Can have a blacklist/whitelist/list of IP addresses to block
Blocks traffic that doesn't meet criteria

questions.text: A company has a network that includes a web server. Customers access the company's web pages that are stored on the web server. Describe how the web pages are located, retrieved and displayed on a customer's computer.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: The users type the URL into the address bar/web browser
The web browser sends the URL to the DNS
The DNS searches for the matching IP address
The DNS returns the IP address to the web browser
If the DNS cannot find the IP address it sends the URL to the next DNS
The web browser sends a request to the IP address/web server
The web server sends the data for the web page to the web browser
The web browser renders the HTML data to display the web page

questions.text: A student has a computer that has a central processing unit (CPU). Describe the role of the CPU in the computer.
A student has a computer that has a central processing unit (CPU). The CPU contains registers. State the purpose of a register.

marking_points.text: To allow immediate access to data during the FDE cycle

questions.text:
A student has a computer that has a central processing unit (CPU). The CPU contains registers. Give three registers that are built into the CPU.
1
2
3
A student has a computer that has a central processing unit (CPU). One component in the CPU is the arithmetic logic unit (ALU). Describe the purpose of the ALU.

marking_points.text: To perform logical operations // by example
To store interim results of calculations

questions.text: A student has a computer that has a central processing unit (CPU). The student wants to replace the CPU to increase the performance of the computer. Explain why a different CPU can increase the performance.

marking_points.text: … increasing the number of FDE cycles/instructions it can perform at the same time
It could have a higher clock speed
… increasing the number of FDE cycles/instructions per second it can perform
It could have a greater cache size
… meaning more frequently used data can be accessed faster

questions.text:
A printer runs out of ink and needs a new ink cartridge. An interrupt is sent and a message is generated to notify the user that the ink cartridge is empty. Complete the paragraph about the use of an interrupt in this process. Use the terms from the list. Some of the terms in the list will not be used. You should only use a term once.

Word list: binary, clock, computer, data, error, fetch–decode–execute cycle, hexadecimal, higher, interrupt queue, interrupt service routine (ISR), lower, printer, priority level, secondary storage, sorting, transmission

The [blank] sends an interrupt to the [blank]. The interrupt is given a [blank]. The processor completes its current [blank] and checks the [blank] to see if there is an interrupt with [blank] priority than the current task. If there is, it stores the current task and fetches the interrupt. It then calls the [blank], which is a sequence of instructions that handles the interrupt. The interrupt is handled, which generates a message to the user to notify them that the ink cartridge is empty.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term in the correct place.

questions.text:
A printer runs out of ink and needs a new ink cartridge. An interrupt is sent and a message is generated to notify the user that the ink cartridge is empty. Handling interrupts is one function of an operating system. Give two functions of an operating system that relate to memory.
1
2
